storyInfo: The trio on the square receive divine revelations in the song of Kereseira's harp. All comes to an end, and the melted snow from the mountains continues to flow, bringing flowers and reeds home.

attributes.content: (It's been a long time since I last felt hot blood flowing in my veins.)
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: (Not since I left the battlefield and left my homeland have I...)
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: His vision begins to blur from the loss of blood. Everything seems covered in a white glow, and he instinctively closes his eyes.
He hears the song of a harp, but he does not know from whence it comes.
He forces his eyes open, and sees himself in an unfamiliar space. He seems to be floating in mid-air, people passing in front of his eyes without acknowledging him.
It is as though he is in a dream.
I will never work with the Sargonians! Beat me, torture me, kill me if you want—
attributes.name: Minoan Captive

attributes.content: But you won't get one word out of me about the guerrillas!
attributes.name: Minoan Captive

attributes.content: So there are guerrillas.
attributes.name: Sargonian Guard

attributes.content: ...Skata!
attributes.name: Minoan Captive

attributes.content: Why are you so angry? I thought Minoans liked logic. That's what the stories of heroes all say.
attributes.name: Sargonian Guard

attributes.content: Are you trying to butter me up? I'm not talking!
attributes.name: Minoan Captive

attributes.content: No need to be so on edge. Just wanted to have a conversation. I don't like interrogating people either.
attributes.name: Sargonian Guard

attributes.content: But I'll be reprimanded if I don't spend enough time in here. Nothing I can do.
attributes.name: Sargonian Guard

attributes.content: What are you doing, Mashayer? Are you so stupid you can't do something as simple as interrogate a prisoner?
attributes.name: Sargonian Officer

attributes.content: I'm on it, sir!
attributes.name: Sargonian Guard

attributes.content: Piece of trash. Some noble. Pah.
attributes.name: Sargonian Officer

attributes.content: The prisoner watches as his interrogator jumps up from the ground nervously, while his superior leaves after casually hurling a few words of abuse.
Both seem used to such a situation. It is as though the officer cusses and abuses the guard on a daily basis.
The guard's complexion is young and pale. He is thin and mild-mannered, very different from the villagers' impression of Sargonians.
The prisoner warns himself not to let his guard down.
Sargonians are cunning. Perhaps the two of them conspired to put on an act.
Well, I have to act like I'm interrogating you.
attributes.name: Sargonian Guard

attributes.content: Could you... say something? Anything.
attributes.name: Sargonian Guard

attributes.content: Anything? I've got a litany of curses for you. I could go on for days.
attributes.name: Minoan Captive

attributes.content: ...Let's talk about something else. Something nicer, something that can help us forget about our struggles and helplessness... tell me some Minoan heroic stories.
attributes.name: Sargonian Guard

attributes.content: I know some of your legends from ancient books that I collected, but they're not easy to find in Sargon.
attributes.name: Sargonian Guard

attributes.content: You already told this story a couple of days ago. I remember.
attributes.name: Sargonian Guard

attributes.content: Tsk.
attributes.name: Minoan Captive

attributes.content: Anything else? Think!
attributes.name: Sargonian Guard

attributes.content: (Weren't we just putting on a show for his superior? Why is he taking such careful notes?)
attributes.name: Minoan Captive

attributes.content: (Why would a Sargonian love Minoan legends so much? Is there something not right with this guy's head?)
attributes.name: Minoan Captive

attributes.content: You've only told a hundred or so. There must be more stories and legends in the land of heroes.
attributes.name: Sargonian Guard

attributes.content: (Trying to provoke me? Not falling for that!)
attributes.name: Minoan Captive

attributes.content: Fragments you heard from the elders as a child are fine. It doesn't need to be complete or popular.
attributes.name: Sargonian Guard

attributes.content: The kind of story that would make a little Sargonian girl dream about Minos... there must be more, right?
attributes.name: Sargonian Guard

attributes.content: (Skata... well, if that's what he wants...)
attributes.name: Minoan Captive

attributes.content: (I'll just make one up. It's not like this idiotis Sargonian could tell.)
attributes.name: Minoan Captive

attributes.content: Alright. Let me tell you about Siopi, the silent hero.
attributes.name: Minoan Captive

attributes.content: It is the strangest experience the prisoner has ever had. No one would believe the story if he told it. He decided to take it with him to his grave—
If he ever gets out of here.
Bloody bother. Finally got him off my back for the day.
attributes.name: Minoan Captive

attributes.content: I didn't know I was so good at making up stories. I should become a bard if I ever get out.
attributes.name: Minoan Captive
Minoan Captive
attributes.content: If I ever get out...

attributes.name: Minoan Captive
attributes.content: Is that a crack in the door?
Did that ilithios forget to lock the door?
attributes.name: Minoan Captive

attributes.content: It has been a long time since he last saw the stars. It almost feels like he is dreaming as he breathes the air of freedom again.
Is he out? Is he free? Just like this?
Useless trash! You've been in that Minoan's cell every single day, and you haven't gotten one scrap of intel about the guerrillas?
attributes.name: Sargonian Officer

attributes.content: S-Sorry, sir. I've been taking records, but he contradicts himself every day. I... I'm afraid nothing he says can be trusted.
attributes.name: Sargonian Guard

attributes.content: I knew you couldn't get anything done! Incompetent fool!
attributes.name: Sargonian Officer

attributes.content: I'll interrogate him tomorrow while you watch and learn how things are done in the lord ameer's army!
attributes.name: Sargonian Officer

attributes.content: Y-Yes, sir...
attributes.name: Sargonian Guard

attributes.content: The fugitive hears the guard and his superior talking in the distance. He hastens his movement through the bushes.
The movement seems to have drawn the attention of the Sargonians.
How did the prisoner get out?!
attributes.name: Sargonian Officer

attributes.content: After him, Mashayer, you worthless shit! You are DEAD, if you don't catch him!
attributes.name: Sargonian Officer

attributes.content: Hearing the sound made by the skinny guard's ill-fitting armor sets the fugitive's heart at ease.
He knows a single Sargonian could never catch him, on this Minoan land.
He takes one final look back as he escapes the Sargonian camp.
The fugitive sees the frail guard stumble and fall. The guard's luxurious armor, probably an heirloom, weighs him down so much that he struggles to get himself off the ground.
Or perhaps... he just wanted an excuse to not get up?
Did he really forget to lock the door, or did he finally find an opportunity to leave a crack?
The prisoner does not know.
The only thing that the prisoner knows is that he will never forget the time he spent at the Sargonian camp, and the ridiculous-looking Sargonian.
(What... is this?)
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: It's my father.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Xanthos turns around in surprise, and sees the Sargonian woman, standing in the same vision as he.
There is surprise on her face, yes, but also nostalgia and poignance.

I know he came to Minos in the lord ameer's army, but was sent back to Sargon for his incompetence. He would have received a heavier punishment, but what little glory the House of Mashayer had left protected him one last time.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: He never really... achieved anything, and it wasn't a surprise that he would mess up after he was conscripted. He didn't like to talk about his time in Minos, and I never asked...
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: I didn't know this was where those stories of the Minoan heroes he told me came from.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: He's very different from my image of a Sargonian.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Yes. He was always weak and soft, never talking back even when insulted to his face... I always wondered why he loved heroic stories so much, when he was the furthest thing from a hero.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: No wonder... no wonder he told me the story of Siopi the silent hero so many times, the hero who kept a secret for thirty years...
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: He planted the seed of heroes in your heart.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: I have high hopes for you, Medjehtiqedti Nasutu Mashayer.
attributes.name: Padishah Khery

attributes.content: It is an honor.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Do you understand why I appointed someone like you to lead the mission, someone so young and inexperienced?
attributes.name: Padishah Khery

attributes.content: You must have your reasons.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: You're much smarter than your father was.
attributes.name: Padishah Khery

attributes.content: The House of Mashayer was a noble house with a rich tradition, and I was on good terms with your elders.
attributes.name: Padishah Khery

attributes.content: It pained me to see the family fall on hard times under your father, who was frail and idle.
attributes.name: Padishah Khery

attributes.content: I arranged a cushy post for him in the lord ameer's army in Minos, safely away from the frontlines, and he almost got himself into a court-martial.
attributes.name: Padishah Khery

attributes.content: When Minos came up again after all these years, you were the first person I thought of.
attributes.name: Padishah Khery

attributes.content: Young Medjehtiqedti is much sharper than her father, I thought. I'm sure she will have no trouble bringing a harp to Minos and bringing back a few blueprints that belonged to Sargon to begin with.
attributes.name: Padishah Khery

attributes.content: You'll do a fine job and do your family proud, won't you?
attributes.name: Padishah Khery

attributes.content: Yes, exalted padishah! I loved the legends of Minos, and I've wanted to see true heroism with my own eyes since I was little. I'll make sure the Minoan mission goes perfectly!
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: The padishah said a bunch of flowery words to me before I left, but I never understood why he made me the head of this mission.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: I guess it's because it's easy to get rid of me, if something goes wrong.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: And I had no idea that those 'blueprints' contained such detailed records.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Frankly, I don't really care about doing the Mashayer name proud.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: I came because I wanted to see this land that I've always been fascinated by with my own eyes, to chase the legends that I've only heard about...
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Or perhaps, I wanted to find some answers about my father...
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: What is this land of heroes that father talked about really like, and what is the heroic spirit really like? I wanted to learn, to find out. That's why I came to Minos.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Have you found the answer?
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Yes. What about you?
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: I was once like you, Xanthos.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Pallas?! You too...
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: I've seen with my own eyes how our people died shielding their comrades from the Sargonians, and heard the screaming of the wounded being amputated in village huts.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Hate flowed in your heart and mine, in the hearts of every Minoan.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Even now... in my heart...
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: I know.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: But have you ever stopped to think why we love to drink?
attributes.name: Pallas
Pallas
attributes.content: Besides the sweet fragrance of wine and the joy of harvest that it represents, the inspiration and joy that it brings us...

attributes.name: Pallas
attributes.content: Besides all that...
It's also because it makes us forget, if only for a while.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Too many things trouble us and make us hurt, but we can't forget them completely, because to do so would be a betrayal, so we drown ourselves in drink, seeking a moment's forgetfulness.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: But then... I had to leave Minos.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: You're Pallas, aren't you? The medics told me you often drink here.
attributes.name: Knightsports Fan

attributes.content: Yes, I am... what is it?
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: You're Minoan, right? You've been in the Lachedamon competitions? Do you know Dikaiopolis, the Blood Knight? Did you ever fight him?
attributes.name: Knightsports Fan

attributes.content: No... but I've heard of him. A Minoan Infected who participates in the Kazimierz Majors, I think?
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Well, actually, the 'competitions' in those two places are not quite the same thing...
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: But they're both clashes of physical ability and skill, aren't they?
attributes.name: Knightsports Fan

attributes.content: Mm... well... uh...
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: I knew it! The principles of strength are the same everywhere! The Blood Knight must be strong because of his Minoan upbringing!
attributes.name: Knightsports Fan

attributes.content: I wanted to meet the Blood Knight, but he's retired now.
attributes.name: Knightsports Fan

attributes.content: Still, it's an honor to meet another Minoan master! Can I have your autograph? Anywhere's fine—how about my t-shirt?
attributes.name: Knightsports Fan

attributes.content: ...(Quietly signs)
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Thank you so much!
attributes.name: Knightsports Fan

attributes.content: So... do Minoans have a special way of training?
attributes.name: Knightsports Fan

attributes.content: I heard you love drinking. Is this part of your training? Accelerating blood circulation with alcohol and increasing explosiveness?
attributes.name: Knightsports Fan

attributes.content: The medics also say you can fall asleep anywhere! This must be a way to save energy! You only fully utilize your physical ability when necessary!
attributes.name: Knightsports Fan

attributes.content: I see! This is why the Blood Knight was so explosive!
attributes.name: Knightsports Fan

attributes.content: Er... these are just my personal habits. I don't know how the Blood Knight trains, but I doubt...
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: I get it, I get it, just fantasizing about things. Did I get carried away? I hope I didn't cause any offense.
attributes.name: Knightsports Fan

attributes.content: ...It's alright. You really love Dikaiopolis, don't you?
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Yeah! I lost all hope after learning that I have Oripathy, but the Blood Knight gave me the will to live again.
attributes.name: Knightsports Fan

attributes.content: I watched recordings of his bouts so many times! I've even started training myself, hoping to join an Infected Knightclub someday.
attributes.name: Knightsports Fan

attributes.content: He was the one who blazed the trail for the Infected in Kazimierzian knightsports, paving the way for the Radiant Knight's return to the tournaments!
attributes.name: Knightsports Fan

attributes.content: His bout with the Radiant Knight was one for the ages! I was there for it, and I cried when they took the podium together.
attributes.name: Knightsports Fan

attributes.content: I wasn't sure why I cried. Maybe it's because I saw a flicker of hope for the future.
attributes.name: Knightsports Fan

attributes.content: I guess he's the kind of hero the Minoans talk about!
attributes.name: Knightsports Fan

attributes.content: He's Infected. According to conventional Minoan wisdom...
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Do Infected heroes have some kind of special title in Minos?
attributes.name: Knightsports Fan

attributes.content: No... But he's a hero, yes. No one can deny it.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Traditions don't stay unchanged forever, Xanthos.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: The stories of the heroes defined and shaped who we are. Yet, because heroes arise from among us, we in turn possess the power to define them and to reshape what it means to be a hero.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: I learned that after I left Minos.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Our goddess of victory is back!
attributes.name: Minoan Villager

attributes.content: I heard you destroyed another Sargon cavalry unit that tried to sneak across the border?
attributes.name: Minoan Villager

attributes.content: I told you not to call me that when we're in the village... it's embarrassing.
attributes.name: Minoan Girl

attributes.content: It was the title that we gave to Pallas...
attributes.name: Minoan Girl

attributes.content: Whoever leads us to victory is our goddess of victory. That's what Pallas said when she left—
attributes.name: Minoan Villager

attributes.content: 'You can use this overblown title however you want, if it scares the Sargonians.'
attributes.name: Minoan Villager

attributes.content: As long as we still have our 'Goddess of Victory', you'll keep the Sargonians away.
attributes.name: Minoan Villager

attributes.content: Yeah... although Sargonian raiders still come every now and then, since she signed that 'Non-Aggression Pact' with Sargon's border tribes on behalf of the villages in the area...
attributes.name: Minoan Girl

attributes.content: The raids have been getting fewer and fewer after we won a few victories. It's all thanks to the reputation of the 'Goddess of Victory'.
attributes.name: Minoan Girl

attributes.content: Besides, inheriting Pallas's title makes me feel like I can't give up, which helps me pull through in tough battles.
attributes.name: Minoan Girl

attributes.content: It doesn't just scare our enemies; it also inspires our own.
attributes.name: Minoan Girl

attributes.content: Yeah, that's what heroes are for.
attributes.name: Minoan Villager

attributes.content: The village of Akroti... brings back so many memories.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: It's very much like my homeland.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: All the villages of Minos are alike. The same soil nurtured us.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: But it's not just because of the same soil that we're a single nation. It's because we know the same stories.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Only we, upon seeing the olive branch, remember how the hero Pheidippides ran from Athenius to spread the news of our victory over the Sargonians to the other cities.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: He was carrying the olive branch of Athenius, and so it became the symbol of victory and hope to us.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: This is what truly belongs to us, something that outlanders and invaders cannot take away.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: As long as we keep living, we'll carry on these stories, and change the traditions so that the legends of Minos's heroes may continue, that the spirit of Minos may continue.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Minos is wherever we are.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: It is a strange scene that unfolds before the eyes of the citizens of Athenius and the guests from Sargon and other city-states.
It is an exhilarating battle. But when the oar-wielding Minoan warrior's blood falls upon the strings of the harp in the hands of Kereseira's statue—
The ancient harp begins to make a sound, like an old machine starting up after a long slumber, its gears slowly beginning to turn.
As the harp begins to sing, the trio on the stage slows down, entering a trance-like state, sometimes angry, sometimes sad, sometimes laughing, sometimes weeping.
When the Minoan challenger is finally overcome by exhaustion and slumps down by the statue's pedestal, the other two on stage finally break out of their trance.
They look around themselves in confusion before running towards the challenger, reaching out to him to try to help him up, but getting no response from him.
The shouting, the cheering, and the angry yelling have all ceased. Those present begin to realize that they have witnessed a miracle.
The harp that stopped singing after the Sargonians invaded has returned to where it belonged, and declared—
The tradition continues, and a new chapter is written in the stories of the heroes.
The harp's music echoes in the sky above the square.
Xanthos looks up.
The autumn sun is painfully bright, but for some reason it seems to bring back dim memories, back to the day that he said goodbye to Ithakos.

attributes.text: Do you believe that the guerrillas can defeat the Sargonians?
Even though we've been losing for a century?

attributes.content: (Nods)
attributes.name: Ferryman

attributes.content: I haven't received any divine revelations, and I can't tell the future. I can't even leave with you.
attributes.name: Ferryman

attributes.content: But I will promise what I can.
attributes.name: Ferryman

attributes.content: I'll protect our village, our houses, our farms and flowerbeds. Perhaps we can go to Athenius, where you came from, once you're victorious.
attributes.name: Ferryman

attributes.content: I'll still be a ferryman, ferrying you to your workplace at the temple on the other side of the city as the sun shines upon the Delphi Canal... I've been imagining the scene ever since you described it to me.
attributes.name: Ferryman

attributes.content: ...Are you listening, Ithakos?
attributes.name: Ferryman

attributes.text: Minos is in for a cold winter this year, brother.

attributes.content: Yes. But the spring will be all the warmer for it.
attributes.name: Ferryman

attributes.content: He finally remembers what he said to Ithakos.
And he remembers the warm smile on Ithakos's face at the answer.
The pain and anger of losing a dear friend had clouded his memory and caused him to forget what was most important.
But things will get better, when spring comes and war is over, when life gets back on track.
The wounds of the earth will heal, and the snowmelt from the peaks of Heria will continue to flow, bringing flowers and reeds home.
